name: Harvest MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Harvest — the popular time tracking and invoicing SaaS. Enables AI agents to log time entries, manage projects and tasks, create invoices, query timesheets, and access Harvest reports. Supports automated time tracking workflows for teams using Harvest for billing and project management.
use_cases: Logging time entries to Harvest from productivity agents based on work sessions
Querying Harvest timesheets and generating billing reports from finance agents
Managing Harvest projects and tasks from project management agents
Automating invoice creation from tracked time in Harvest
not_for: Teams using Toggl, Clockify, or other time tracking tools
Fully automated time tracking without human review for billing (financial accuracy matters)
best_when: A freelancer, agency, or team using Harvest for time tracking wants AI agents to log time, query timesheets, and automate billing workflows.
avoid_when: You don't use Harvest. Also: time entries drive real invoicing — review agent-logged time before billing clients.

auth.notes: Harvest OAuth 2.0 or personal access token. Harvest developer account required for OAuth app registration.

pricing.notes: Harvest has a free tier for solo users. Team use requires paid subscription. MCP server is free open source.

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Time entries directly drive client invoicing — agent errors can affect billing accuracy
Harvest rate limit is 100 requests/15 seconds — batch operations can hit this quickly
Time entries require project_id and task_id — agent must have valid IDs for logging
Review agent-logged time before generating invoices to avoid billing disputes
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Time entries affect client billing. OAuth 2.0 with scopes. Harvest data includes client billing info — treat as sensitive.

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0. Billing data sensitive. Time entry integrity important for invoicing accuracy.
